# CST-Python

CST-Python is a Python module of the CST, the Cognitive Systems Toolkit, a toolkit for the construction of cognitive systems and cognitive architectures.

This project was developed as part of the Cognitive Architectures research line from  the Hub for Artificial Intelligence and Cognitive Architectures (H.IAAC) of the State University of Campinas (UNICAMP). See more projects from the group [here](https://github.com/brgsil/RepoOrganizer).

[![](https://img.shields.io/badge/-H.IAAC-eb901a?style=for-the-badge&labelColor=black)](https://hiaac.unicamp.br/)
[![](https://img.shields.io/badge/-Arq.Cog-black?style=for-the-badge&labelColor=white&logo=data:image/svg%2bxml;base64,)](https://github.com/brgsil/RepoOrganizer)

## Repository Structure

- src: cst_python source code
- tests: code tests


## Installation / Usage

- Installing using pip:

    ```bash
    pip install --upgrade pip
    pip install cst_python
    ```

- For installing from the repository:

    ```bash
    git clone https://github.com/H-IAAC/CST-Python
    cd CST-Python
    pip install --upgrade pip
    pip install .
    ```

classes.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_container.MemoryContainer
documentation:
This class represents a Memory Container. The Memory Container is responsible
for implementing an important element in the Dynamic Subsumption mechanism
used in CST. All the Memory Objects in a Container are of the same type, and
hold the same parameters. The only differences among them are that they were
generated by a different codelet, and they might have different evaluations.
An evaluation is an inner parameter from any Memory Object, which holds a
value (usually a real value between 0 and 1) that measures a relative
importance given by the codelet, and which is used by the Evaluation codelet
within the Container to decide which from all input Memory Objects will be
sent to the output.

classes.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.__init__
documentation: Creates a MemoryObject.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.update_info
documentation:
Args:

: info (Any): the info in memory object to set.

:::{deprecated} Use set_info() instead
:::

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.timestamp
documentation: Date when the data was "created" in milliseconds.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.timestamp
documentation: @alias.alias("setTimestamp")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.__str__
documentation: @alias.alias("toString", "to_string")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.__hash__
documentation: @alias.alias("hashCode", "hash_code")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory_object.MemoryObject.__eq__
documentation: @alias.alias("equals")

A Memory Object is a generic information holder, acting as a sign or an
internal representation, which is responsible to store any auxiliary or
perennial information necessary for the cognitive architecture to perform its
behavior. Codelets and Memory Objects are intrinsically coupled to each
other, in the sense that Memory Objects hold the information necessary for
the Codelets to run, and are also the placeholders of any new information
generated by the codelet. The main property being hold by a Memory Object is
its Information (I). This information can be simply a number, or hold complex
structures like lists, trees, graphs or whole networks. In our computational
implementation, the information I is a generic Java Object, which can be used
to represent virtually anything. A Memory Object also has two extra kinds of
meta-information: a time stamp (T), which tags the Memory Object with a
marker indicating its last update, and an evaluation (E), which has many
different uses within CST. This evaluation is simply a number, which can be
used, e.g. as an appraisal factor in an emotional module, or simply as a
discriminator to differentiate among two or more Memory Objects of the same
type. These meta-information can be simply ignored by simpler codelets, or be
useful while implementing more sophisticated cognitive models.

Memory that contains the environment reset.

Memory that contains the step count for the current environment

documentation: Reset the indexes for setting the sufix of new memories.

methods.name: cst_python.python.gym.gym_codelet.GymCodelet.space_to_memories
documentation:
Creates memories from a gym Space definition.

Args:
    mind (Mind): mind to create the memories.
    space (gym.Space): space defining the memories to create.
        If gym.space.Dict, creates a memory for each element, 
        creates a single memory otherwise.
    action (bool, optional): If True, creates a memory with 'action' 
        name for non Dict space, uses 'observation' name otherwise. 
        Defaults to False.
    memory_prefix (Optional[str], optional): prefix to memories name. 
        Defaults to None.

Returns:
    dict[str, MemoryObject]: created memories, indexed by the space
        element name or 'action'/'observation'.

methods.name: cst_python.python.gym.gym_codelet.GymCodelet.sample_to_memories
documentation:
Writes a gym.Space sample to memories.

Args:
    sample (Mapping[str, Any] | Any): sample to write in the memories.
    memories (Mapping[str, Memory]): memories corresponding to 
        the space elements.

methods.name: cst_python.python.gym.gym_codelet.GymCodelet.memories_to_space
documentation:
Convert the memories info to the space sample.

Args:
    memories (Mapping[str, Memory]): memories to get the sample.
    space (gym.Space): space the sample belongs

Raises:
    ValueError: if the generated sample from the memories 
        doesn't belongs to the space

Returns:
    dict[str, Any]|Any: converted sample.

Codelet to interface with gymnasium/gym environments. Creates memories for the observation, 
action, reward, reset, terminated, truncated, info and seed; and updates them stepping the 
environment with the action.

classes.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet.__init__
documentation:
MemoryStorageCodelet initializer.

Args:
    mind (Mind): agent mind, used to monitor memories.
    node_name (Optional[str], optional): name of the local node in the network.
        If None, creates a unique name with 'node{int}'. Defaults to None.
    mind_name (Optional[str], optional): name of the network mind. 
        If None, uses 'default_mind'. Defaults to None.
    request_timeout (float, optional): time before timeout when
        requesting a memory synchonization. Defaults to 500e-3.

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet.update_memory
documentation:
Updates a memory, sending or retrieving the memory data
to/from the database.

Performs a time comparison with the local data and storage
data to decide whether to send or retrieve the data.

Args:
    memory_name (str): name of the memory to synchonize.

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet._send_memory
documentation:
Sends a memory data to the storage.

Args:
    memory (Memory): memory to send.

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet._retrieve_memory
documentation:
Retrieves a memory data from the storage.

Blocks the application, it is advisable to use a separate thread to call the method.

Args:
    memory (Memory): memory to retrieve data.

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet._request_memory
documentation:
Requests another node to send its local memory to storage.

Args:
    memory_name (str): name of the memory to request.
    owner_name (str): node owning the memory.

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet._handler_notify_transfer
documentation:
Handles a message in the notify transfer channel.

Args:
    message (dict[str,str]): message received in the channel.

methods.name: cst_python.memory_storage.memory_storage.MemoryStorageCodelet._handler_transfer_memory
documentation:
Handles a message in the transfer memory channel.

Args:
    message (dict[str,str]): message received in the channel.

Synchonizes local memories with a Redis database. 

When using MemoryStorage, each local CST instance is called a node.
Memories with the same name in participating nodes are synchronized.

The collection of synchonized nodes is a mind.
A single Redis instance can support multiple minds with unique names

classes.name: cst_python.core.entities.coalition.Coalition
documentation:
A Coalition is a group of Codelets which are gathered in order to perform a
task by summing up their abilities or to form a specific context.

In CST, two codelets belong to the same coalition when they share information
- pragmatically, when they write in and read from the same memory object.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.memory.Memory.compare_name
documentation:
Compares tha name of this memory with another.

Comparation is case insensitive.

Args:
    other_name (str): name of the other memory.

Returns:
    bool: True if is the same name.

This class represents the interface for all kinds of memories that exist in
CST. In order to be recognized as a Memory, an entity must implement this
interface. Currently, there are to kinds of Memory: MemoryObject and
MemoryContainer. However, other forms of Memory might come up as CST
develops.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.raw_memory.RawMemory.create_rest_memory_object
documentation:
Creates a new RestMemory and adds it to the Raw Memory, using provided
name, hostname and port .

Args:
    name (str): memory object type.
    port (int): the port of the REST server
    hostname (Optional[str], optional): the hostname of the REST server. If None, 
uses 'localhost'. Defaults to None.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: method is not implemented.

Returns:
    RESTMemoryObject: created MemoryObject.
@alias.alias("createRESTMemoryObject")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.raw_memory.RawMemory.create_rest_memory_container
documentation:
Creates a new RestMemory and adds it to the Raw Memory, using provided
name, hostname and port .

Args:

    port (int): the port of the REST server
    hostname (Optional[str], optional): the hostname of the REST server. If None, 
uses 'localhost'. Defaults to None.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: method is not implemented.

Returns:
    RESTMemoryContainer: created MemoryObject.
@alias.alias("createRESTMemoryContainer")

documentation:
MemoryBuffer is a generic holder for memory objects. It may be used to
produce sensory buffers, action buffers or other very short term memory
structures.

A codelet is a priori enabled to run its proc(). However, if it tries to
read from a given output and fails, it becomes not able to do so.
@alias.alias("get_enabled", "getEnabled")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.enabled
documentation: @alias.alias("set_enabled", "setEnabled")

@alias.alias("get_name", "getName")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.name
documentation: @alias.alias("set_name", "setName")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.activation
documentation: Activation level of the Codelet. Ranges from 0.0 to 1.0.
@alias.alias("get_activation", "getActivation")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.activation
documentation: @alias.alias("set_activation", "setActivation")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.inputs
documentation: Input memories, the ones that are read.
@alias.alias("get_inputs", "getInputs")

@alias.alias("get_outputs", "getOutputs")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.outputs

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.threshold
documentation:
Threshold of the codelet, which is used to decide if it runs or not. If
    activation is equal or greater than activation, codelet runs
    proc(). Ranges from 0.0 to 1.0.
@alias.alias("get_threshold", "getThreshold")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.threshold
documentation: @alias.alias("set_threshold", "setThreshold")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.time_step
documentation:
If the proc() method is set to be called automatically in a loop, this
    variable stores the time step for such a loop. A timeStep of value 0
    means that the proc() method should be called continuously, without
    interval.
@alias.alias("get_time_step", "getTime_step")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.time_step
documentation: @alias.alias("set_time_step", "setTime_step")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.broadcast
documentation: Input memories, the ones that were broadcasted.
@alias.alias("get_broadcast", "getBroadcast")
Problem: get_broadcast method overload

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.broadcast
documentation: @alias.alias("set_broadcast", "setBroadcast")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.is_memory_observer
documentation: Defines if codelet is a memory observer (runs when memory input changes).

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.access_memory_objects
documentation:
This method is used in every Codelet to capture input, broadcast and
    output MemoryObjects which shall be used in the proc() method. This
    abstract method must be implemented by the user. Here, the user must get
    the inputs and outputs it needs to perform proc.
@alias.alias("accessMemoryObjects")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.calculate_activation
documentation:
This abstract method must be implemented by the user. Here, the user must
calculate the activation of the codelet before it does what it is
supposed to do in proc().
@alias.alias("calculateActivation")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.proc
documentation: Main Codelet function, to be implemented in each subclass.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.run
documentation:
When first activated, the thread containing this codelet runs the proc()
method.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.start
documentation: Starts this codelet execution.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.stop
documentation: Tells this codelet to stop looping (stops running).

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.impending_acess
documentation:
Safe access to other Codelets through reentrant locks.

Args:
    accessing (Codelet): the Codelet accessing.

Raises:
    NotImplementedError: this method is not implemented yet.

Returns:
    bool: True if is impeding access.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet._get_memory
documentation:
This method returns an memory from a list. If it couldn't
find the given M, it sets this codelet as not able to perform proc(), and
keeps trying to find it.

Can search by the memory's name, or type and index.

Args:
    search_list (List[Memory]): list to search memories.
    type (Optional[str], optional): type of memory it needs. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.
    index (Optional[int], optional): position of memory in the sublist. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.
    name (Optional[str], optional): the name of the memory being searched. If None, searches by the type and index. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Memory|None: the memory searched or None if not found.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.get_input
documentation:
This method returns an input memory from its input list. If it couldn't
    find the given M, it sets this codelet as not able to perform proc(), and
    keeps trying to find it.

Args:
    type (Optional[str], optional): type of memory it needs. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.
    index (Optional[int], optional): position of memory in the sublist. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.
    name (Optional[str], optional): the name of the memory being searched. If None, searches by the type and index. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Memory|None: the memory searched or None if not found.
@alias.alias("getInput")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.get_output
documentation:
This method returns an output memory from its output list. If it couldn't
    find the given M, it sets this codelet as not able to perform proc(), and
    keeps trying to find it.

Args:
    type (Optional[str], optional): type of memory it needs. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.
    index (Optional[int], optional): position of memory in the sublist. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.
    name (Optional[str], optional): the name of the memory being searched. If None, searches by the type and index. Defaults to None.

Returns:
    Memory|None: the memory searched or None if not found.
@alias.alias("getOutput")

    index (Optional[int], optional): position of memory in the sublist. If None, searches by the name. Defaults to None.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.codelet.Codelet.notify_codelet
documentation: Runs when codelet is a memory observer and memory input changes
@alias.alias("notifyCodelet")

The **Codelet** class, together with the **MemoryObject**
class and the **Mind** class is one of the most important classes
in the CST toolkit. According to the Baars-Franklin architecture,
consciousness is the emergence of a serial stream on top of a parallel set of
interacting devices. In the Baars-Franklin architectures, such devices are
called "codelets", which are small pieces of code specialized in performing
simple tasks. In a CST-built cognitive architecture, everything is either a
**Codelet** or a **MemoryObject**. Codelets are used to
implement every kind of processing in the architecture.

Codelets have two kinds of inputs: standard inputs and broadcast inputs.
Standard inputs are used to convey access to MemoryObjects. Broadcast inputs
come from consciousness, and can also be used. Nevertheless, Standard inputs
are usually fixed (but can be changed through learning mechanisms), and
Broadcast inputs change all the time, due to the consciousness mechanism.
Codelets also have outputs. Outputs are used for the Codelets to write or
generate new MemoryObjects. Codelets also have an Activation level, which can
be used in some situations.
TODO: Profile, Broadcast, impending access, correct exception types
@alias.aliased

documentation:
Creates a codelet and adds it to this coderack.

Args:

Returns:
    Codelet: the own codelet inserted, if it is needed to concatenate to
        further methods calls.
@alias.alias("insertCodelet")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.code_rack.CodeRack.create_codelet
documentation:
Creates a codelet and adds it to this coderack.

Args:
    activation (float): codelet's activation.
    broadcast (List[Memory]): list of memory objects which were broadcast lately (treated as
        input memories).
    inputs (List[Memory]): list of input memories.
    outputs (List[Memory]): list o output memories.
    codelet (Codelet): codelet to be created.

Returns:
    Codelet: the codelet created.
@alias.alias("createCodelet")

documentation:
Removes a codelet from coderack.

    codelet (Codelet): the codelet to be destroyed.
@alias.alias("destroyCodelet")

documentation: Destroys all codelets. Stops CodeRack's thread.
@alias.alias("shutDown", "shut_down")

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.code_rack.CodeRack.start
documentation: Starts all codelets in coderack.

methods.name: cst_python.core.entities.code_rack.CodeRack.stop
documentation: Stops all codelets within CodeRack.

Following Hofstadter and Mitchell
"The copycat project: A model of mental fluidity and analogy-making". Pool of
all alive codelets in the system. The whole arena in the Baars-Franklin
metaphor.
